lifecycles._note: budget is a GLOBAL (account-scoped, no region) financial-management service (see CONTEXT.md global-services list). Closes all 3 budget writes: POST /v1/budgets/account (createaccountbudget), PUT /v1/budgets/account/{budget_id} (setaccountbudget), DELETE /v1/budgets/account/{budget_id} (deleteaccountbudget). An account budget is a non-billable spend-alert config (no infra), but a shared account may already have one and the body is UNPROVEN (no data/api_bodies.json entry; docs JS-rendered), so this is COVERAGE-ONLY guarded: every write is optional + broad expect_status so a 4xx/409 (e.g. a budget already exists, or missing required field) still records coverage without failing the run. budget_id is captured from a 2xx create when possible, else soft-captured from the read-only list so the PUT/DELETE/GET-by-id still target a real id. Bodies (amount/threshold/notification fields) are best-effort from field semantics. Covers GAP-get-id showaccountbudget via probe_reads/show step too.

steps.name: create-account-budget
steps._note: Creates an account spend-alert budget. Body (budget_amount/currency/period_type/notifications) is best-effort, UNPROVEN. Capture path $.budget.id is a guess; soft so a different envelope still records coverage (the list-captured id covers PUT/DELETE). Expected 409 if a budget already exists on the shared account.

steps.name: show-account-budget
steps._note: Read-only id-bound GET on the captured/soft-captured budget id (closes GAP-get-id showaccountbudget).

steps.name: set-account-budget
steps._note: In-place update (setaccountbudget). Targets the captured/soft-captured id. Body UNPROVEN. optional so a 4xx records coverage.

steps.name: delete-account-budget
steps._note: destructive — only runs under SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE. Deletes the account budget. Guarded; on a shared account this may target a soft-captured pre-existing budget so it is optional and never required to succeed (expected 403/404).
